title: iOS Package
documentation:
## Purpose
IPA (iOS App Store Package) is the binary distribution format for iOS, iPadOS, and macOS (on Apple Silicon) applications. Structurally a ZIP archive, it contains a `.app` bundle with Mach-O executables, resources, and Apple-signed metadata. The [iOS Package](https://capdag.com/browse/media:ipa;archive) media type represents any `.ipa` file.

## When to use
Use this type when a node produces or consumes iOS application binaries — for example, CI/CD build pipelines, App Store submission workflows, enterprise distribution systems, or security analysis toolchains. Distribution requires Apple code signing; unsigned IPA files will not run on real devices.

## Notes
IPA files can be opened with any ZIP library. The app bundle is located at `Payload/<AppName>.app/`. The main executable is a Mach-O fat binary (ARM64). Implementers that inspect or re-sign IPA files should use `codesign` (macOS) or `zsign` and must re-sign after any modification. The `;archive` tag indicates this is a packaged archive artifact.
